title: Liquid Chromatography-Tandem Mass Spectrometry (LC-MS/MS) Acquisition by Data-Independent Acquisition (DIA) on an Orbitrap Eclipse Tribrid Mass Spectrometer

Thermo Fisher Scientific, PN 164570)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":20,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6tih2","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"et6l5","text":"Autosampler vials and caps","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5a2it","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a55pb","text":"LC-MS-grade water","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1shg3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aob3q","text":"Indexed retention time peptides (iRT, Biognosys)\n","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"atr8i","text":"Solvent A: 2% acetonitrile (ACN), 0.1% formic acid (FA) in water","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ffv0h","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2pgg","text":"Solvent B: 98% acetonitrile (ACN), 0.1% formic acid (FA) in water","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Dilute the peptide sample with solvent A (2% ACN, 0.1% FA) to the desired concentration. Transfer the samples to an autosampler vial and add iRT peptides to the sample according to the manufacturer’s instructions.

Inject the sample onto a Dionex UltiMate 3000 liquid chromatographic system coupled to an Orbitrap Eclipse Tribrid mass spectrometer and load the peptides on a C 18 trap column over 10 min at 5 µL/min with 100% solvent A.
Elute the peptides with a C 18 analytical column heated at 35°C and at a flow rate of 300 nL/min over 215 min using the following gradient of solvent B:

Acquire the data in data-independent acquisition (DIA) mode using the settings described in Table 1 . One cycle consists of one full MS scan collected in the Orbitrap analyzer followed by DIA-MS/MS scans collected in the Orbitrap analyzer based on an isolation scheme of 26 variable windows covering the 350 - 1,650 m/z range with an overlap of 1 m/z ( Table 2 ). Table 1. Settings for data-independent acquisition on an Orbitrap Eclipse Tribrid mass spectrometer. Table 2. Isolation scheme of the data-independent acquisition method.
